metadata.name: Costa Rica ICE Transmission Network (Simplified)
metadata.description: 8-node simplified representation of the Instituto Costarricense de Electricidad (ICE) high-voltage transmission network. Nodes represent major generation centers and substations. Edge weights represent isolation benefit based on line length (km) and fault exposure.
metadata.source: Topology derived from ICE open data portal (datos-ice-se.opendata.arcgis.com) and public transmission maps. Weights are representative approximations.

nodes.name: Arenal
nodes.description: Arenal hydroelectric complex — largest reservoir in Costa Rica

nodes.name: Miravalles
nodes.description: Miravalles geothermal field — renewable baseload generation

nodes.name: Cañas
nodes.description: Cañas substation — major transmission hub in Guanacaste

nodes.name: Garabito
nodes.description: Garabito thermal plant — peaking/backup capacity near Pacific coast

nodes.name: San José
nodes.description: San José metropolitan area — primary load center, ~40% national demand

nodes.name: Moín
nodes.description: Moín substation — Caribbean coast, port infrastructure load

nodes.name: Palmar
nodes.description: Palmar Sur substation — Southern Pacific region, interconnection point

edges.description: Arenal–Miravalles: connects two major renewable generation centers
Arenal–Cañas: hydroelectric output to Guanacaste hub
Miravalles–Cañas: geothermal output to Guanacaste hub
Cañas–Garabito: long Pacific corridor, high fault exposure
Garabito–San José: critical feed to main load center
San José–Cachí: Central Valley to hydroelectric generation
Cachí–Moín: cross-mountain Caribbean corridor, high vulnerability
San José–Moín: alternative Caribbean route through Braulio Carrillo
Garabito–Palmar: Southern Pacific backbone, longest line
